source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure unused ENIs are removed

input_row.source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure unused ENIs are removed
input_row.description: Identify and delete any unused Amazon AWS Elastic Network Interfaces in order to adhere to best practices and to avoid reaching the service limit. An AWS Elastic Network Interface (ENI) is pronounced unused when is not attached anymore to an EC2 instance.
input_row.audit: From the Console: 1. Login to EC2 using https://console.aws.amazon.com/ec2/ 2. On the left Click NETWORK & SECURITY, click Network Interfaces. 3. Select the ENI that you want to review 4. Go to the Details tab 5. Check the value set for the Status attribute 6. If it says available, refer to the remediation below. 7. Repeat steps 3 - 6 to determine the current status for any other ENIs within the current region. NOTE Repeat the audit process for all other regions used. From the CLI 1. Run describe-network-interfaces command aws ec2 describe-network-interfaces --region us-east-1 --output json -- filters Name=status,Values=available --query "NetworkInterfaces[*].{ENI:NetworkInterfaceId}" 2. The command output should return an empty list if the default security group is not being used. 3. If there is a list of ENI IDs then refer to the remediation below. 4. Repeat steps 1 - 3 to determine the current status for any other ENIs within the current region. NOTE Repeat the audit process for all other regions used.
input_row.remediation: From the Console: 1. Login to EC2 using https://console.aws.amazon.com/ec2/ 2. On the left Click NETWORK & SECURITY, click Network Interfaces. 3. Select the ENI that you want to remove 4. Click 'Actions', then 'delete' 5. Click Delete 6. Repeat steps 3 - 5 any other ENIs listed in the audit within the current region. NOTE Repeat the audit process for all other regions used. From the CLI 1. Run the delete-network-interface command with the ENI names collected above in the audit. aws ec2 delete-network-interface --region us-east-1 --network-interface-id eni-1234abcd 2. This will remove the ENI that is not being used. 3. Repeat steps 1 - 2 for any ENIs within the current region. NOTE Repeat the audit process for all other regions used.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control pertains to checking the status of Elastic Network Interfaces (ENIs) to determine if they are unused.
- The audit instructions explicitly mention using the AWS CLI command `aws ec2 describe-network-interfaces` to check the status of ENIs.
- The status of ENIs is a standard AWS property that can be queried via AWS APIs.
- Boto3 SDK provides methods to interact with network interfaces, such as `describe_network_interfaces`, which can be used to automate the check.
- There is no requirement for manual judgment, SSH access, or an application-level inspection, making it suitable for automation.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_ec2_network_interface_unused_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the boto3 SDK or AWS CLI to enumerate all ENIs in each AWS region.
- API Call: `ec2.describe_network_interfaces()` with a filter for `Status=available` to identify unused ENIs.
- CLI Command: `aws ec2 describe-network-interfaces --filters Name=status,Values=available --query "NetworkInterfaces[*].{ENI:NetworkInterfaceId}"`
- Iterate over all AWS regions to ensure the check is comprehensive.
- Validate that the output is an empty list to confirm that no unused ENIs are present.
- Pass/Fail Criteria:
  - Pass: If no ENIs with `Status=available` are found in any region.
  - Fail: If any ENI with `Status=available` is identified, indicating an unused ENI.
- Report the IDs of any unused ENIs found for further action.
